title: The Most Creative Posts From Pittsburgh Small Businesses
subtitle: Judged by Claude on visual concept, caption craft, format fit, and surprise. Not ranked by likes.
angle: the IG posts that took risks, made craft choices, or said something specific this quarter, ranked by editorial judgment instead of follower count.
intro:
Every quarter, we ask a simple question: which Pittsburgh small businesses are actually doing something interesting on Instagram, not just posting to post. This list rewards craft. A juice bar with 3,000 followers can outscore a regional chain with half a million if the concept is sharper, the caption earns its place, and the format does real work. Follower counts don't appear anywhere in our scoring. Execution does.

This quarter's top 30 pulled from 17 businesses, and the range was genuinely surprising. Lorelei landed the top spot by doing something most accounts never attempt: matching its mid-century aesthetic so precisely to its actual concept that the $1 hot dog and live music post felt less like marketing and more like a scene you'd already missed. Page's, in second, ran an April Fools misdirection so convincingly staged it drove the kind of engagement most real announcements never see. Golden Age Beer Company, third, let a vintage neon sign and a specific mention of Czech lager do the heavy lifting, which turns out to be more than enough.

Issue 02 expands the scoring panel and adds a new category tracking how well posts hold up one week after publish.

items.caption: Mondays are about to be a vibe at Lorelei.  Starting this Monday, we are pleased to bring you Mid-Century Mondays in the beer hall.  Our talented pals from the Pleaktones will be serving up live 60s lounge music, while we sling beers, wines

scores.why: Retro aesthetic perfectly matches the mid-century concept, voice is genuinely fun and specific, and $1 hot dogs plus live music create authentic local appeal.

items.why: Retro aesthetic perfectly matches the mid-century concept, voice is genuinely fun and specific, and $1 hot dogs plus live music create authentic local appeal.
